title: Giving directions to a taxi driver
description: Flag down a red taxi and direct the driver to your destination
context: Hong Kong taxis are colour-coded: red for urban areas, green for the New Territories, blue for Lantau. Red taxis are the most common. Drivers appreciate it if you know your destination and can give cross-street landmarks. Always have the Chinese characters for your destination ready.
cultural_note: Hong Kong taxis are colour-coded by territory: red cabs operate on Hong Kong Island and Kowloon; green cabs cover the New Territories; blue cabs serve Lantau Island only. A red taxi cannot legally pick up a passenger in Sha Tin and vice versa. Crossing boundaries incurs extra tolls, which is why drivers sometimes refuse passengers heading to the wrong zone. Sit in the back, not beside the driver.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, heoi3 tung4 lo4 waan1.
lines.english: Excuse me, to Causeway Bay please.

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: heoi3 tung4 lo4 waan1 bin1 dou6?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 去時代廣場附近，謝斐道。
lines.jyutping: heoi3 si4 doi6 gwong2 coeng4 fu6 gan6, ze6 fei1 dou6.

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: hou2, haang4 hung4 seoi6 ding6 hai6 sai1 seoi6?
lines.english: OK. Cross Harbour Tunnel or Western Harbour Tunnel?

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: hou2, dou3 gaa3 laa3, jat1 baak3 jat1 sap6 man1.
lines.english: Here we are, one hundred and ten dollars.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 呢度一百二十蚊，唔使找喇。
lines.jyutping: ni1 dou6 jat1 baak3 ji6 sap6 man1, m4 sai2 zaau2 laa3.
lines.english: Here's one hundred and twenty, keep the change.
